verses.commentary: Peter addresses elders (presbyteros, πρεσβύτερος): leaders, overseers. He appeals as fellow elder, witness of Christ's sufferings, and one who will share in the glory to be revealed. Authority comes from shared experience and future hope, not position. Pastoral leadership requires humility and eschatological perspective.

Be shepherds (poimainō, ποιμαίνω) of God's flock. The flock belongs to God, not the shepherd. Watch over them (episkopeo, ἐπισκοπέω): oversee, care for. Not because you must but because you are willing, as God wants. Not for dishonest gain but eager to serve. Motivation matters: compulsion versus willing service, greed versus devotion.

Not lording it over (katakyrieuo, κατακυριεύω) those entrusted to you. Leadership is not domination. Be examples (typos, τύπος): models, patterns. Leaders lead by being, not just doing. Character precedes competence. Christlike humility, not worldly authority.

When the Chief Shepherd (archipoimēn, ἀρχιποίμην) appears, you will receive the crown of glory (stephanos, στέφανος) that will never fade. Christ is the Chief Shepherd; human shepherds are undershepherds. Faithful service is rewarded eternally. Earthly ministry has eschatological significance.

In the same way, younger ones submit (hypotassō, ὑποτάσσω) to elders. All of you clothe yourselves (egkomboomai, ἐγκομβόομαι): tie on like a servant's apron. Humility toward one another. Proverbs 3:34 quoted: God opposes the proud but shows favor to the humble. Humility attracts grace; pride invites opposition.

Humble yourselves (tapeinoō, ταπεινόω) under God's mighty hand. Humility is active, not passive. That He may lift you up in due time (kairos, καιρός): the appointed time. God exalts the humble at the right moment. Self-promotion is unnecessary; God's timing is perfect.

Cast (epirriptō, ἐπιρρίπτω) all your anxiety on Him because He cares for you. Casting is decisive action: throw your burdens on God. Anxiety is incompatible with trust. God's care is the basis for releasing control. He is able and willing to carry what crushes us.

Be alert (nēphō, νήφω): sober, clear-minded. Be of sober mind (grēgoreō, γρηγορέω): watchful, vigilant. Your enemy the devil prowls (peripateo, περιπατέω) like a roaring lion seeking someone to devour (katapinō, καταπίνω). Spiritual warfare is real. The devil is predator, not myth. Vigilance is essential.

Resist (anthistēmi, ἀνθίστημι) him, standing firm in the faith. Resistance is active, not passive. Standing firm (stereos, στερεός): solid, steadfast. The family of believers throughout the world undergoes the same sufferings. You are not alone in persecution. Suffering is global, not isolated. Solidarity strengthens endurance.

The God of all grace called you to His eternal glory in Christ. After you have suffered a little while, He will restore (katartizō, καταρτίζω): mend, perfect. Make you strong (sthēnizō, σθηνίζω), firm (sterizō, στηρίζω), steadfast (themelioo, θεμελιόω): established, grounded. Suffering is temporary; glory is eternal. God completes what He begins.

To Him be the power (kratos, κράτος) forever and ever. Amen. Doxology: ascribing dominion to God. He is sovereign over all, including suffering. Worship is the proper response to God's faithfulness through trials.

With the help of Silas (Silvanus), whom I regard as a faithful brother, I have written briefly. Silas was Paul's companion (Acts 15:40); now he assists Peter. Encouraging you and testifying that this is the true grace of God. Stand fast in it. The letter's purpose: encouragement and confirmation. Grace is not theory but lived reality.

She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, sends greetings. Babylon likely refers to Rome (Revelation 17:5, 18:2), the center of imperial power and persecution. Chosen together: fellow elect. My son Mark: John Mark, author of the Gospel of Mark. Restored after earlier failure (Acts 15:37-39). Church is family.

Greet one another with a kiss of love. Holy kiss (Romans 16:16, 1 Corinthians 16:20). Cultural expression of affection and unity. Peace to all of you who are in Christ. Peace (eirēnē, εἰρήνη): shalom, wholeness, well-being. True peace is found only in union with Christ.
